claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by gathering some basic information:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex, who's 9 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm assessing eligibility for your son Alex, who is 9 years old.

Let me ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** By this I mean - does Alex live with you, or do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards the cost of looking after him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me, but I do contribute towards his upkeep. I help out with money for his clothes, school expenses, and other things he needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex doesn't live with you but you do contribute towards his upkeep, I need to understand a bit more about the situation.

**Do you contribute at least the equivalent of what Child Benefit would pay (which is currently around £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child) towards Alex's costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute that amount. I send £30 a week for Alex's needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

Now, this is an important question: **Does anyone else currently receive Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, does the person Alex lives with claim Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about Alex's living situation:

**Who does Alex currently live with?** And is this a permanent arrangement, or is it temporary (such as being in hospital or in care for a short period)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with his mum - my ex-partner. It's a permanent arrangement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. This is helpful for understanding the situation.

Now I need to ask: **Does Alex's mum contribute to his upkeep as well?** I'm asking because when a child doesn't live with you, the priority for Child Benefit goes to the person who has the main responsibility for the child - which is usually the person the child lives with, or the person who contributes more towards their costs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she does contribute - she's the one looking after him day-to-day since he lives with her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining the situation. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- Alex is 9 years old (under 16, so age-eligible)
- Alex lives permanently with his mum (your ex-partner)
- His mum looks after him day-to-day and contributes to his upkeep
- You contribute £30 per week towards Alex's costs
- No one is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Unfortunately, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex in this situation.** 

Here's why: When a child doesn't live with you, Child Benefit priority goes to the person who has the main responsibility for the child. Since Alex lives with his mum and she provides his day-to-day care, she has the main responsibility for him - even though you also contribute financially.

**Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any one time**, and in cases where the child lives with someone else, that person (in this case, Alex's mum) has the priority claim.

**However, Alex's mum would be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex** if she wishes to do so, as she is the person with main responsibility for him.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that. It's disappointing but I understand - since Alex lives with his mum, she would be the one who should claim it. I appreciate you taking the time to go through everything with me.
